clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Standardized selection and dosing of antihypertensive agents for urgent BP control in pregnant patients
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Severe hypertension in pregnancy requiring urgent blood pressure reduction (not eclampsia seizure prophylaxis)
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Agent selection + dosing protocol + safety screening

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for urgent BP management
Screen for contraindications (asthma, heart block, cardiac dysfunction)
Choose from Labetalol, Hydralazine, or Nifedipine
Calculate weight-based or fixed dosing with cumulative max
Select IV push, infusion, IM, or oral route
Track expected onset of action (1-20 min depending on agent)
Monitor for adverse effects (hypotension, tachycardia, headache, fetal HR changes)
Determine if repeat dose needed based on BP response

validationRules: Check contraindications before labetalol: no asthma, no heart block, no cardiac dysfunction, no bradycardia

rules: If patient has asthma, heart block, cardiac dysfunction, or bradycardia → avoid labetalol
If hydralazine dose >20 mg cumulative → monitor closely for maternal hypotension and abnormal fetal heart rate
If nifedipine administered → monitor for reflex tachycardia and headache
Agent selection based on contraindications, onset time needed, and route availability
Track cumulative dosing against maximum thresholds
Redosing interval based on agent-specific timing (10-30 min for labetalol, 20-40 min for hydralazine, 20 min for nifedipine)

agents.name: Nifedipine
agents.redosing: repeat in 20 min if needed, then 10-20 mg every 2-6 h

category.description: e.g., 'Labetalol - First line for most' or 'Hydralazine - Alternative' or 'Nifedipine - Oral option'

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Consider magnesium sulfate for seizure prophylaxis, escalate to maternal-fetal medicine
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Continue urgent BP control with selected agent, monitor BP q15min

appDisplay.behavior: Strict dose tracking with maximum alerts

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Notify obstetrics team and maternal-fetal medicine
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor BP every 15-30 minutes until controlled
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Recheck BP at peak onset time for each agent
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Alert if cumulative dose exceeds maximum or adverse effects occur
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log agent, dose, route, BP response, and adverse effects to maternal health record
